steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims that IGF-1 gene variations "must account for" size differences in dogs — implying it is the sole explanation. The question asks which choice most directly undermines that conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'IGF-1 is one of multiple genes that affect size': directly undermines "must account for" — if other genes also contribute, one gene alone cannot explain all size variation. ✓
  • Choice B — 'the finding may not generalize across species': addresses generalizability beyond dogs, which is irrelevant to the student's claim about dogs specifically. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the IGF-1 gene is important for size': supports the student's conclusion rather than undermining it. ✗
  • Choice D — 'variant prevalence among breeds': discusses how common a variant is, not whether IGF-1 alone explains size differences. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
